title: Warm Croatian Cheese Gratin
description: A mustardy, cheese-pull indulgence featuring elderflower glaze, baked in a tandoor clay pot.
instructions: Preheat oven to 200°C and place a tandoor clay pot inside to heat.
While the pot warms, grate the aged cheddar and Gruyere into a bowl.
In a saucepan over medium heat, combine heavy cream, mustard, elderflower syrup, butter, oil, salt, and pepper; stir until butter melts and mixture is smooth.
Remove the pot from the oven, pour the creamy mixture over the cheeses, and stir gently until the cheeses begin to melt.
Sprinkle the flour and egg yolk over the mixture, stirring until evenly incorporated.
Place the pot back in the oven and bake for 20 minutes, or until the top is golden and the cheese is bubbling.
Remove the pot carefully, brush the top with a thin layer of elderflower-mustard glaze, and return to the oven for 5 minutes to develop a lacquered finish.
Serve immediately, garnishing with a sprinkle of fresh black pepper if desired.
